items.title: Vehicle carrying released Palestinian prisoners leaves West Bank's Ofer Prison
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Israel began releasing 369 Palestinian prisoners and detainees on Saturday, including 36 serving life sentences over deadly attacks. The first released prisoners arrived by bus in a town in the West Bank and were greeted by a cheering crowd.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Lebanese army helps to control demonstrators after UN peacekeeping force convoy comes under attack
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The outgoing deputy commander of the UN peacekeeping force in Lebanon was injured Friday after protesters attacked a convoy taking peacekeepers to the Beirut airport, UNIFIL said in a statement.

items.title: Displaced Palestinians in Gaza fearful war may resume, as they struggle to secure food and shelter
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Displaced Palestinians in central Gaza remain fearful that the war could resume, longing for stability as they struggle to find shelter and secure food. Some 30 people lined up in front of a charity kitchen in Deir al-Balah, holding containers and pots to grab their share of yellow rice and small meat chunks.  (AP Video shot by Abdel Kareem Hana; Production by Wafaa Shurafa) 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Three more hostages handed over by Hamas-led militants in Gaza
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Three more Israelis held captive in the Gaza Strip were freed Saturday as part of a shaky ceasefire deal that requires Israel to release hundreds of Palestinian prisoners in exchange. 
Militants paraded Iair Horn, 46, a dual citizen of Israel and Argentina; American-Israeli Sagui Dekel Chen, 36; and Russian-Israeli Alexander (Sasha) Troufanov, 29 — before a crowd before releasing them.
Crowds in Hostages Square in Tel Aviv jumped and cheered as they watched live.
(AP video from Gaza shot by: Mohammad Jahjouh)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Crowd gathers as militants prepare to release more hostages
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Three more Israelis held captive in the Gaza Strip are on the verge of being freed by Hamas-led militants as part of a shaky ceasefire deal that requires Israel to release hundreds of Palestinian prisoners in exchange. The truce that began nearly four weeks ago had been jeopardized in recent days by a tense dispute that threatened to renew the fighting.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Deadly border stop in Vermont reveals web of violence in 3 states tied to cult like group
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The killing of a U.S. Border Patrol agent near the Canadian border last month has been linked to a cultlike group at the center of five other homicides in three states. (AP Video: Rodrique Ngowi, Matt Rourke, Terry Chea. Production: Rodrique Ngowi.)

items.title: LIVE: Palm Beach County hosts Valentine’s Day group wedding
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Watch live as Palm Beach County Clerk Joseph Abruzzo hosts a Valentine’s Day group wedding. Twenty-five couples are expected to tie the knot at the historic 1916 Palm Beach County Courthouse.

#valentinesday #palmbeach #florida #live</article>

items.title: 'There is no safety': Palestinian family returns to Gaza City to find home in ruins
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A family in central Gaza were overjoyed to return to their home after moving from place to place at least seven times during the war between Israel and Hamas. But the relief and joy has given way to shock and despair after discovering what was left of their home following 15 months of fighting.  (AP video shot by Mohammad Jahjouh; Production by Wafaa Shurafa).

#gaza #palestinian #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Rihanna and A$AP Rocky exit courthouse together
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Rihanna and A$AP Rocky exit a Los Angeles courthouse Friday surrounded by photographers after Rocky's assault case went to jurors, who will begin deliberations Tuesday. #rihanna #asaprocky #trial</article>

items.title: A$AP Rocky trial LIVE: Closing arguments set to finish
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Watch live as A$AP Rocky's lawyer is expected to finish delivering his closing argument at the hip-hop star's trial, where he is charged with firing a gun at a former friend on a Hollywood street in 2021. Read more: https://bit.ly/4k9KJtU 

#asaprocky #live</article>

items.title: What is Daytona After Dark?
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>After the big race is over, Daytona 500 fans enjoy their night on the Daytona International Speedway infield, wheelbarrow racing, performing karaoke and enjoying each other’s company. #shorts #DaytonAfterDark #Daytona500 #NASCAR</article>

items.title: Where US adults think the government is spending too much and too little
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Many U.S. adults believe the federal government is overspending. But polling also shows many Americans, including Republicans, think the country is spending too little on major government programs like Social Security. Read more: https://bit.ly/3X1cefk 

#news

items.title: What to know about the Trump administration’s sweeping layoffs of probationary workers
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Trump administration has intensified its sweeping efforts to shrink the size of the federal workforce by ordering agencies to lay off nearly all probationary employees. Here's what to know.

#news #uspolitics #trump #layoffs</article>

items.title: Why Pope Francis has been admitted to the hospital
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Pope Francis was hospitalized Friday after a weeklong bout of bronchitis worsened and was receiving drug therapy for a respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said. It's the latest setback to the 88-year-old’s pontiff’s health and that forced him to cancel his agenda through Monday. #shorts #Pope #PopeFrancis #Vatican</article>
